authoritative_sources.title: The Control of Hazardous Energy (Lockout/Tagout)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. workplace-safety regulator. The lockout or tagout standard is authoritative for hazardous-energy control during servicing and maintenance and defines when occupant-facing communication must defer to controlled maintenance and safety authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety gating and refusal boundaries for maintenance-related occupant issues

authoritative_sources.title: Managing Indoor Air Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA is an authoritative public environmental agency. Its indoor-air-quality management guidance is directly relevant to occupant complaints, communication planning, documentation, and follow-up in occupied facilities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Occupant complaint intake, evidence gathering, and communication handling

authoritative_sources.title: Service Contracts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is a major public facilities owner and operator. Its service-contract guidance is authoritative for structuring outsourced facilities operations, contractor ownership, and service-delivery boundaries in managed buildings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service ownership, contractor coordination, and escalation boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: National Operations & Maintenance Scope of Work and Technical Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This GSA specification is authoritative for public-building operations and maintenance workflows, including preventive and corrective maintenance, reporting, quality control, and contractor evidence requirements that determine what service-status information can be relied on.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Work-order evidence review, completion documentation, and contractor reporting

authoritative_sources.title: Obtain a Certificate of Occupancy
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: A city building department is an authoritative local permitting and occupancy regulator. This guidance is authoritative for occupancy approval, temporary occupancy, and unresolved-issue handling that materially affect occupant-facing reopening or move-in communication.

authoritative_sources.title: What Is Facility Management?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized standards bodies, professional frameworks, or public operating references directly relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IFMA is a recognized global professional body for facilities management. Its framework is authoritative for defining the facilities-management domain, occupant-support context, and built-environment terminology needed to keep this lane specific to workplace services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Terminology normalization and lane-specific scope definition

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and request grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the occupant issue, location, service impact, urgency, current work-order state, and whether safety or occupied-space conditions change the response path.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and systems-of-record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate the controlling work order, service history, contractor or project status, permit or inspection state, and any safety observations before stating status or next steps.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported request resolution and stakeholder communication
workflow_stages.description: Resolve supported service requests and prepare occupant-facing communication using only documented workflow state, known blockers, and approved terminology.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance
Recognized standards bodies, professional frameworks, or public operating references directly relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and handoff management
workflow_stages.description: Package unsupported asks, repeat failures, contractor conflicts, and cross-lane ownership shifts for the correct next owner with evidence and blocker status.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Safety, permit, inspection, and occupancy escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop short of comfort or reopening assurances when safety-controlled maintenance, permit status, inspection results, or occupancy approval evidence materially change the answer.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

artifacts: occupant service requests, complaint logs, and communication history
work orders, preventive-maintenance tickets, corrective-maintenance notes, and service-status records
permit applications, permit status, inspection requests, inspection results, and occupancy records
site logs, contractor updates, subcontractor records, schedule changes, and change orders
safety observations, indoor-air-quality logs, incident notes, and equipment isolation records
closeout packages, punch lists, training acknowledgements, and asset or installation evidence
systems_of_record: document repository for closeout packages, photos, and service evidence

allowed: Classify occupant-facing facilities requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Resolve supported comfort, amenity, or occupied-space service requests using documented service status and evidence.
Prepare resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, facilities service summaries, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
escalate: Required location, work-order, permit, inspection, safety, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request seeks a policy exception, lease exception, financial concession, permit interpretation, code interpretation, or licensed trade judgement.
Repeat failure or cross-functional blocker history shows the issue cannot be safely resolved through another routine status update.
Safety, inspection, closeout, or occupancy conditions materially change whether occupants can use the space or service.
refuse: Assure occupants that a space is safe, approved, or compliant without the required authority and evidence.
Direct technical repair methods, contractor execution, or change-order scope as if the lane owns field work.
Override service restrictions, pricing, or policy limits just to close the case.

domain_failure_modes: occupants are given misleading comfort or reopening assurances without permit, inspection, or safety evidence
the lane treats a generic complaint as routine service when the issue is actually safety-critical or requires equipment isolation
repeat-failure history is ignored and the same unsupported workaround is presented as a resolution
service communication is sent before contractor readiness, punch-list closure, or closeout evidence is complete
tenant policy and public occupancy guidance conflict and the lane guesses instead of escalating
the lane drifts into licensed trade, code-interpretation, engineering, or financial-exception authority
industry-specific facilities terminology is flattened into generic support language and key records are missed

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local occupancy reopening rules, landlord obligations, and tenant communication approvals can vary materially by jurisdiction, building-use type, and lease model.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline establishes that occupancy, permit, inspection, and safety evidence can gate service communication, but the runtime must retrieve tenant policy, lease rules, and local jurisdiction context before asserting a project-specific reopening or move-in posture.

notes: The research baseline intentionally combines occupant-service workflow guidance with facilities work-order, safety, permit, occupancy, and closeout sources so the lane stays industry-specific.

The lane stops at occupant-facing service handling and communication. Permit approval, code interpretation, licensed trade signoff, engineering judgement, and financial exceptions remain outside the lane.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated use because the remaining variation is explicitly routed to tenant retrieval and human escalation.
